title: toona microcarpa var. pilipetala
intro: Toona microcarpa var. pilipetala, commonly known as the Philippine meranti, is a deciduous tree species native to the Philippines. It is a type of tree that belongs to the mahogany family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The extracts from the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, and have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The herbal preparations of Toona microcarpa var. pilipetala include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
